claims.claim: Prospeo's SOM benchmark assigns 1% of SAM to Year 1, 3% to Year 2, and 5% to Year 3
claims.sourceTitle: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM): How to Calculate It
claims.excerpt: SOM benchmarks that won't get you laughed out of the room: 1% of SAM in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 5% in Year 3.

claims.claim: Prospeo defines SOM as revenue realistically captured over 1-3 years, not a single-year figure
claims.sourceTitle: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM): How to Calculate It
claims.excerpt: SOM is the revenue you can realistically capture in 1-3 years. Not a fantasy percentage of SAM.

claims.claim: IdeaPlan states early-stage companies typically capture 1-5% of SAM as SOM
claims.excerpt: For early-stage companies, SOM is typically 1-5% of SAM.

claims.claim: IdeaPlan states growing companies with product-market fit can reach 10-20% of SAM
claims.excerpt: For growing companies with product-market fit, SOM can reach 10-20% of SAM.

claims.claim: IdeaPlan states market leaders in well-defined categories may achieve 30%+ of SAM, and winner-take-most market leaders can exceed 40%
claims.excerpt: Market leaders in well-defined categories may achieve 30%+ of SAM. These percentages vary by market structure: in winner-take-most markets (like cloud infrastructure), the leader can capture 40%+ of SAM

claims.claim: IdeaPlan defines SOM as the portion of SAM capturable typically within one to three years, not specifically Year 1
claims.excerpt: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM) is the portion of the Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM) that a company can realistically capture in the near term, typically within one to three years.

claims.claim: Wise defines SOM as the realistic share of SAM expected to be captured over three to five years, not one year
claims.sourceTitle: Calculating market size with TAM, SAM and SOM for startups - Wise
claims.excerpt: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM): The realistic share of SAM you expect to capture in the next three to five years, accounting for competition and your execution capacity.

claims.claim: TryBuildCo's worked example yields SOM of $3.2M against SAM of $3.2B — a 0.1% ratio, describing 'year 1-2' capture
claims.sourceTitle: How to Calculate TAM SAM SOM for Your Startup (With Real Examples) | TryBuildCo

claims.claim: TryBuildCo frames SOM as 'What you'll actually capture year 1-2'
claims.sourceTitle: How to Calculate TAM SAM SOM for Your Startup (With Real Examples) | TryBuildCo
claims.excerpt:
SOM — Serviceable Obtainable Market
What you'll actually capture year 1-2

claims.claim: WorthBuild cites the pitch-deck cliché of claiming 1% of a large market as a red flag investors dislike
claims.sourceTitle: How to Calculate SOM (Serviceable Obtainable Market) for Your Startup — Blog | WorthBuild
claims.excerpt: You've probably heard the pitch deck cliché: "If we capture just 1% of this $50 billion market…" Investors hear this dozens of times a week, and it makes most of them cringe.

claims.claim: Tractian's revenue grew from $200K to $32.2M over four years, not a discrete first-year figure
claims.sourceTitle: Tractian: How Two Sons of Factory Engineers Built a $32M Business on Sensors That Predict Breakdowns — Solopreneur
claims.excerpt: The company grew from $200K to $32M in revenue over four years and serves more than 1,000 plants, including John Deere, Caterpillar, and Goodyear.

claims.claim: Tractian's addressable predictive-maintenance market is cited at $14.9B by 2026
claims.sourceTitle: Tractian: How Two Sons of Factory Engineers Built a $32M Business on Sensors That Predict Breakdowns — Solopreneur
claims.excerpt: Market $14.9 млрд к 2026 году (предиктивное обслуживание, по данным MarketsandMarkets)

claims.claim: Deploy 95 reports 30% of deployed industrial hardware systems were never fully accepted by customers, requiring uninstallation
claims.sourceTitle: The Economics of POC - by Trista Li - Deploy 95
claims.excerpt: Thirty percent of deployed systems were never fully accepted: customers would sign, we'd deploy, and six months later, it came back. We were sending teams to uninstall while trying to hit revenue targets.

tensions.text: Multiple SOM definitions (Prospeo, IdeaPlan, Wise, TryBuildCo) describe the obtainable-share percentage as accruing over a 1-3 or 3-5 year window, not specifically 'Year 1' — the node's anchoring of the 1% figure strictly to Year 1 may overstate precision the sources don't actually support at single-year granularity.

TryBuildCo's worked example produces an SOM/SAM ratio of ~0.1% ($3.2M/$3.2B), which falls below the node's stated low end of 0.5%, suggesting the 0.5-3% sensitivity band may not bracket all realistic obtainable-share outcomes.

The node's cross-check cites 'Tractian comparable (first-year ~$1.2M against a multi-$B market)' but the attached Tractian source only reports starting revenue of $200K growing to $32M over four years — no explicit first-year $1.2M figure appears in the evidence, so the cross-check number is unverified against the cited source.

absences: No industry-specific (DC electrical gear, rack-PDU, or predictive-maintenance hardware) Year-1 obtainable-share benchmark was found — only generic SaaS/startup SOM heuristics (1-5%, 10-20%, 30%+) appear across sources.

No first-year revenue or market-share figures for comparable early-stage hardware entrants (only Tractian's aggregated four-year trajectory from $200K to $32M) were located to validate a Year-1-specific capture rate.
No quantification of how the cited 12-24 month early-procurement lock-in cycle for DC electrical gear translates into a bounded Year-1 obtainable-share percentage.

No competitor-level market-share breakdown (e.g., for Cognex, Keyence, Matrox, FANUC, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa, or rack-PDU vendors) was found that would allow a bottom-up validation of the 1% Year-1 assumption.
